texto: An education is truly “fitted for freedom” only if it is such as to produce free citizens, citizens who are free not because of wealth or birth, but because they can call their minds their own. Male and female, slave-born and freeborn, rich and poor, they have looked into themselves and developed the ability to separate mere habit and convention from what they can defend by argument. They have ownership of their own thought and speech, and this imparts to them a dignity that is far beyond the outer dignity of class and rank.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Martha C. Nussbaum sobre temas ligados a educacao. A afirmação “An education is truly “fitted for freedom” only if it is such as to produce free citizens, citize…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, education, freedom, útil em momentos de termino e freedom.

texto: As we tell stories about the lives of others, we learn how to imagine what another creature might feel in response to various events. At the same time, we identify with the other creature and learn something about ourselves.
explicacao: Citação de Martha C. Nussbaum sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “As we tell stories about the lives of others, we learn how to imagine what another creature might…” articula reflexao, empathy, about em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: To be a good human being is to have a kind of openness to the world, an ability to trust uncertain things beyond your own control, that can lead you to be shattered in very extreme circumstances for which you were not to blame. That says something very important about the condition of the ethical life: that it is based on a trust in the uncertain and on a willingness to be exposed; it’s based on being more like a plant than like a jewel, something rather fragile, but whose very particular beauty is inseparable from that fragility.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Martha C. Nussbaum: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “To be a good human being is to have a kind of openness to the world, an ability to trust uncertai…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, beauty, trust pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de vida e trust.

texto: It seems to me that good philosophy will always have a place in the investigation of any matter of deep human importance, because of its commitment to clarity, to carefully drawn distinctions, to calm argument rather than prejudice and dogmatic assertion""Philosophical Interventions" (Reviews 1986-2011)
